content.title: Passage 1
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了卢米埃兄弟在巴黎的Capucines大道上开设了他们的Cinematographe，于1895年12月8日，超过100年前，吸引了100名付费观众，让他们惊叹不已地看到照片在平面屏幕上栩栩如生地动起来。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了电影的初次出现对人们产生的震撼和魔力，以及电影作为20世纪最具动态和有效的艺术形式的独特催眠品质。\n简短总结：电影的初次出现对人们产生了巨大的震撼和魔力，使其成为20世纪最具动态和有效的艺术形式。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了卢米埃兄弟早期的一部电影作品，展示了一个阳光洒满的火车站，一列火车直冲镜头而来，俄罗斯导演安德烈·塔可夫斯基将这部电影描述为“天才之作”，并认为这是电影诞生的时刻，观众因为混淆现实与影像而感到恐慌。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了早期电影观众经历了相同的困惑，但电影从未失去其独特的魔力，它能够吸引观众并将他们带入一个不同的世界。对于塔可夫斯基来说，电影的魔力在于它能够创造出真实事件的动态图像，捕捉到真实客观的时间流动。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了电影的现实主义效果是教育世界认识自己，因为电影使世界变得更小，人们在去美国或其他地方之前就已经知道其他地方的样子，知道其他人的工作和生活方式，而且在电影虚构中所记录的生活主要是美国的，从电影产业的早期开始，好莱坞就主导了世界电影市场，美国的形象成为电影的主要形象，电影将美国的生活和价值观传播到全球。', 'F': '电影记录了现代世界的生活细节，使得未来的后代能够更加深入地了解20世纪，比其他时期更加亲近，我们将比任何前代更为人所知。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了电影明星的产生和他们在观众心中的超现实形象，以及电影明星作为放大的人类自我在电影界的奇特和持久的遗产。', 'H': '这段话主要讲述了电影给故事的概念带来了新的生机，当卢米埃兄弟和其他先驱开始展示这一新发明时，人们并不清楚它将如何被使用，最初只关注的是运动的奇迹，有人甚至说，一旦这种新奇感消失，电影就会逐渐消失，它只是一个过眼云烟的噱头，一个游乐场的吸引物。', 'I': '这段话主要讲述了电影发展的可能性以及最终成为讲故事的媒介的历史过程。\n简短总结：电影最终成为讲故事的主要媒介，最早是短篇故事，后来发展为长篇小说式的叙事形式。', 'J': '这段话主要讲述了电影的诞生对人们的冲击和改变，以及人们对电影所带来的新奇和真实感的认知。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The Lumière Brothers opened their Cinematographe at 14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris, to 100 paying customers over 100 years ago, on December 8, 1895. Before the eyes of the stunned, thrilled audience, photographs came to life and moved across a flat screen.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: So ordinary and routine has this become to us that it takes a determined leap of the imagination to grasp the impact of those first moving images. But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: One of the Lumière Brothers' earliest films was a 30-second piece which showed a section of a railway platform flooded with sunshine. A train appears and heads straight for the camera. And that is all that happens. Yet the Russian director Andrei Tarkovsky, one of the greatest of all film artists, described the film as a 'work of genius'. 'As the train approached,' wrote Tarkovsky, 'panic started in the theatre: people jumped and ran away. That was the moment when cinema was born. The frightened audience could not accept that they were watching a mere picture. Pictures were still, only reality moved; this must, therefore, be reality. In their confusion, they feared that a real train was about to crush them.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Early cinema audiences often experienced the same confusion. In time, the idea of film became familiar, the magic was accepted—but it never stopped being magic. Film has never lost its unique power to embrace its audiences and transport them to a different world. For Tarkovsky, the key to that magic was the way in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events. A still picture could only imply the existence of time, while time in a novel passed at the whim of the reader. But in cinema, the real, objective flow of time was captured.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: One effect of this realism was to educate the world about itself. For cinema makes the world smaller. Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived. Overwhelmingly, the lives recorded—at least in film fiction—have been American. From the earliest days of the industry, Hollywood has dominated the world film market. American imagery—the cars, the cities, the cowboys—became the primary imagery of film. Film carried American life and values around the globe.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: And, thanks to film, future generations will know the 20th century more intimately than any other period. We can only imagine what life was like in the 14th century or in classical Greece. But the life of the modern world has been recorded on film in massive, encyclopaedic detail. We shall be known better than any preceding generations.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The 'star' was another natural consequence of cinema. The cinema star was effectively born in 1910. Film personalities have such an immediate presence that, inevitably, they become super-real. Because we watch them so closely and because everybody in the world seems to know who they are, they appear more real to us than we do ourselves. The star as magnified human self is one of cinema's most strange and enduring legacies.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Cinema has also given a new lease of life to the idea of the story. When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention, it was by no means obvious how it would be used. All that mattered at first was the wonder of movement. Indeed, some said that, once this novelty had worn off, cinema would fade away. It was no more than a passing gimmick, a fairground attraction.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Cinema might, for example, have become primarily a documentary form. Or it might have developed like television—as a strange, noisy transfer of music, information and narrative. But what happened was that it became, overwhelmingly, a medium for telling stories. Originally these were conceived as short stories—early producers doubted the ability of audiences to concentrate for more than the length of a reel. Then, in 1912, an Italian 2-hour film was hugely successful, and Hollywood settled upon the novel-length narrative that remains the dominant cinematic convention of today.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: And it has all happened so quickly. Almost unbelievably, it is a mere 100 years since that train arrived and the audience screamed and fled, convinced by the dangerous reality of what they saw, and, perhaps, suddenly aware that the world could never be the same again—that, maybe, it could be better, brighter, more astonishing, more real than reality.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-5

Reading Passage 1 has ten paragraphs, A-J.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct answer.

questions.title: the location of the first cinema
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是段落A，因为段落A明确提到了第一家电影院的位置，即“14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris”。题目中的线索词“location”和“first cinema”在段落A中分别对应“14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris”和“Cinematographe”。通过这些关键词的匹配，可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The Lumière Brothers opened their Cinematographe, at 14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris, to 100 paying customers over 100 years ago, on December 8, 1895.
central_sentences.translation: 100多年前，也就是1895年12月8日，卢米埃尔兄弟在巴黎卡普辛大道14号为100名付费观众开设了他们的"电影放映机"。

target_sen.target_sen: The Lumière Brothers opened their Cinematographe, at 14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris, to 100 paying customers over 100 years ago, on December 8, 1895.
The Lumière Brothers opened their Cinematographe, at 14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris, to 100 paying customers over 100 years ago, on December 8, 1895.

questions.title: how cinema came to focus on stories
questions.analysis: 段落I明确描述了电影如何从展示动作转变为讲述故事的媒介，这与题干中的“how cinema came to focus on stories”直接对应。段落I中的句子“it became, overwhelmingly, a medium for telling stories”清楚地解释了这一转变过程，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: But what happened was that it became, overwhelmingly, a medium for telling stories.
central_sentences.translation: 但实际发生的是，电影压倒性地成为了一种讲述故事的媒介。

target_sen.target_sen: Then, in 1912, an Italian 2-hour film was hugely successful, and Hollywood settled upon the novel-length narrative that remains the dominant cinematic convention of today.
But what happened was that it became, overwhelmingly, a medium for telling stories.
Originally these were conceived as short stories - early producers doubted the ability of audiences to concentrate for more than the length of a reel.

questions.title: the speed with which cinema has changed
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落J，因为段落J中明确提到了电影变化的速度：“it has all happened so quickly”和“it is a mere 100 years”。这些表达直接对应了题干中的“the speed with which cinema has changed”。

central_sentences.sentence: And it has all happened so quickly. Almost unbelievably, it is a mere 100 years since that train arrived and the audience screamed and fled, convinced by the dangerous reality of what they saw, and, perhaps, suddenly aware that the world could never be the same again - that, may be, it could be better, brighter, more astonishing, more real than reality.
central_sentences.translation: 这一切发生得如此之快。几乎难以置信的是，距离那列火车到来，观众尖叫着逃离，被他们所看到的危险现实所震撼，并可能突然意识到世界再也不会一样了——也许，它可能会变得更好、更明亮、更令人惊叹、比现实更真实，仅仅过去了100年。

target_sen.target_sen: And it has all happened so quickly.
And it has all happened so quickly.

questions.title: how cinema teaches us about other cultures
questions.analysis: 段落E详细描述了电影如何通过展示其他地方和人们的生活方式来教育观众了解其他文化。这与题干中的“how cinema teaches us about other cultures”直接对应。因此，段落E是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: One effect of this realism was to educate the world about itself. For cinema makes the world smaller. Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived.
central_sentences.translation: 这种现实主义的一个效果是教育世界了解自己。因为电影让世界变小了。早在人们前往美国或其他地方之前，他们就已经知道其他地方是什么样子的；他们知道其他人是如何工作和生活的。

target_sen.target_sen: One effect of this realism was to educate the world about itself.
Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived.
Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived.

questions.title: the attraction of actors in films
questions.analysis: 题干中的“the attraction of actors in films”可以拆解为“attraction”和“actors”两个部分。在段落G中，“immediate presence”和“super-real”描述了演员的吸引力，而“star”和“film personalities”则指代了电影中的演员。因此，段落G最直接对应了题干中的信息。

central_sentences.sentence: Film personalities have such an immediate presence that, inevitably, they become super-real.
central_sentences.translation: 电影人物具有如此直接的存在感，以至于他们不可避免地变得超现实。

target_sen.target_sen: Film personalities have such an immediate presence that, inevitably, they become super-real.
Film personalities have such an immediate presence that, inevitably, they become super-real.
The 'star' was another natural consequence of cinema.
The cinema star was effectively born in 1910.
The star as magnified human self is one of cinema's most strange and enduring legacies.

groups.desc:
Questions 6-9

Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: It is important to understand how the first audiences reacted to the cinema.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是YES。题干中的“important”在原文中被替换为“worth trying”，“understand”被替换为“to understand”，“first audiences”被替换为“initial shock”和“panic started in the theatre”，“reacted”被替换为“panic started”和“people jumped and ran away”。这些同义替换词在原文中明确指出了理解最初观众反应的重要性，因此答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.
central_sentences.translation: 但这值得尝试，因为理解那些最初画面带来的震撼，就是理解电影的非凡力量和魔力，这种独特、令人着迷的品质使电影成为20世纪最富有活力和效果的艺术形式。

target_sen.target_sen: But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.

But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.
So ordinary and routine has this become to us that it takes a determined leap of the imagination to grasp the impact of those first moving images.

But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.

But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.
So ordinary and routine has this become to us that it takes a determined leap of the imagination to grasp the impact of those first moving images.

questions.title: The Lumière Brothers' film about the train was one of the greatest films ever made.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“greatest films ever made”表示“有史以来最伟大的电影之一”，而原文中提到的是Tarkovsky称这部电影为“work of genius”，这只是一个人的观点，并没有明确说这是“one of the greatest films ever made”。因此，题干中的信息在文章中并没有被明确提到，所以答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Yet the Russian director Andrei Tarkovsky, one of the greatest of all film artists, described the film as a 'work of genius'.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，俄罗斯导演安德烈·塔可夫斯基，作为最伟大的电影艺术家之一，将这部电影描述为"天才之作"。

target_sen.target_sen: Yet the Russian director Andrei Tarkovsky, one of the greatest of all film artists, described the film as a 'work of genius'.

questions.title: Cinema presents a biased view of other countries.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“biased view”在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词。虽然文章提到电影主要传播了美国的生活和价值观，但并没有明确提到这是有偏见的描述。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: One effect of this realism was to educate the world about itself. For cinema makes the world smaller. Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived.
central_sentences.translation: 这种现实主义的一个效果是教育世界了解自己。因为电影使世界变小了。早在人们去美国或其他地方旅行之前，他们就知道其他地方是什么样子；他们知道其他人是如何工作和生活的。

target_sen.target_sen: For cinema makes the world smaller.
Overwhelmingly, the lives recorded - at least in film fiction - have been American.
From the earliest days of the industry, Hollywood has dominated the world film market.
American imagery - the cars, the cities, the cowboys - became the primary imagery of film.
Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived.

questions.title: Storylines were important in very early cinema.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Storylines were important in very early cinema.”意思是“故事情节在早期电影中很重要”。然而，原文中明确指出“最初重要的是运动的奇迹”，而不是故事情节。因此，题干中的陈述与原文内容相反，正确答案是“NO”。

central_sentences.sentence: All that mattered at first was the wonder of movement.
central_sentences.translation: 最初最重要的是动态影像的奇妙之处。

syn_ary.synonym: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention

target_sen.target_sen: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention, it was by no means obvious how it would be used.
All that mattered at first was the wonder of movement.

questions.title: The writer refers to the film of the train in order to demonstrate
questions.analysis: 作者提到火车的电影是为了展示早期电影对观众的巨大冲击力。文章描述了观众对火车电影的反应，特别是他们的恐慌和逃跑，显示了早期电影的巨大冲击力。因此，正确答案是B: the impact of early films。

central_sentences.sentence: As the train approached,' wrote Tarkovsky, 'panic started in the theatre: people jumped and ran away. That was the moment when cinema was born. The frightened audience could not accept that they were watching a mere picture. Pictures were still, only reality moved; this must, therefore, be reality. In their confusion, they feared that a real train was about to crush them.
central_sentences.translation: 塔可夫斯基写道："当火车靠近时，剧院里开始出现恐慌：人们跳起来逃走。那就是电影诞生的时刻。受惊的观众无法接受他们只是在看一幅画面。画面是静止的，只有现实才会动；因此，这一定是现实。在他们的困惑中，他们害怕一辆真实的火车即将碾压他们。"

target_sen.target_sen: Yet the Russian director Andrei Tarkovsky, one of the greatest of all film artists, described the film as a 'work of genius'.
Yet the Russian director Andrei Tarkovsky, one of the greatest of all film artists, described the film as a 'work of genius'.

questions.title: In Tarkovsky's opinion, the attraction of the cinema is that it
questions.analysis: 塔可夫斯基认为电影的吸引力在于它能够捕捉到时间的真实流动。这与选项C "illustrates the passing of time" 完全对应。文章中提到 "the real, objective flow of time was captured"，这与选项C的意思一致。

central_sentences.sentence: For Tarkovsky, the key to that magic was the way in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events. A still picture could only imply the existence of time, while time in a novel passed at the whim of the reader. But in cinema, the real, objective flow of time was captured.
central_sentences.translation: 对塔可夫斯基来说，电影魔力的关键在于它能够创造出真实事件流动的动态影像。静态图片只能暗示时间的存在，而小说中的时间则随读者的意愿而流逝。但在电影中，真实、客观的时间流动被捕捉下来了。

target_sen.target_sen: For Tarkovsky, the key to that magic was the way in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events.
In time, the idea of film became familiar, the magic was accepted -but it never stopped being magic.
For Tarkovsky, the key to that magic was the way in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events.

questions.title: When cinema first began, people thought that

options.text: it should be used in fairgrounds.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D: its future was uncertain。文章中明确提到 "it was by no means obvious how it would be used" 和 "some said that, once this novelty had worn off, cinema would fade away"，这些句子表明了电影刚开始时，人们对其未来的用途和发展并不确定，甚至认为它可能只是一个短暂的噱头。

central_sentences.sentence: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention, it was by no means obvious how it would be used. All that mattered at first was the wonder of movement. Indeed, some said that, once this novelty had worn off, cinema would fade away. It was no more than a passing gimmick, a fairground attraction.
central_sentences.translation: 当卢米埃兄弟和其他先驱开始展示这项新发明时，人们并不清楚它将如何被使用。起初，人们只关注运动画面的奇妙之处。事实上，有人说一旦这种新奇感消失，电影就会逐渐消亡。它不过是一种短暂的噱头，一种游乐场的娱乐项目。

syn_ary.synonym: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention

target_sen.target_sen: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention, it was by no means obvious how it would be used.

syn_ary.synonym: it was by no means obvious

target_sen.target_sen: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention, it was by no means obvious how it would be used.
Indeed, some said that, once this novelty had worn off, cinema would fade away.

questions.title: What is the best title for this passage?

options.text: The rise of the cinema star
The power of the big screen

questions.analysis: 文章的核心主题是电影的影响力和魔力，这在多处段落中都有提及。特别是第二段明确提到“extraordinary power and magic of cinema”，这与题目中的“the power of the big screen”直接对应。因此，D选项是最能概括文章主旨的标题。

central_sentences.sentence: Film has never lost its unique power to embrace its audiences and transport them to a different world.
central_sentences.translation: 电影从未失去其独特的力量，它能够吸引观众并将他们带入一个不同的世界。

content.title: Motivating Employees under Adverse Conditions
content.summary: {'A': '高成就者适合担任小型企业或大型企业内部自治单位的工作，而在大型官僚组织中担任管理职位的候选人应具备高权力需求和低从属需求。因此，高成就者不应被安排到与他们需求不一致的工作中，他们在工作中最好能提供适度具有挑战性的目标，并具备独立性和反馈。然而，需要记住并非每个人都受到独立性、多样性和责任感较高的工作的激励。', 'B': '高目标设定理论的文献表明，经理应确保所有员工都有具体的目标，并获得关于目标完成情况的反馈。对于那些具有高成就需求的人来说，通常在任何组织中都只占少数，外部目标的存在就不那么重要了，因为高成就者已经有内在动机。下一个要确定的因素是目标是由经理分配还是与员工共同设定。答案取决于目标接受度和组织文化的认知。如果预计会有对目标的抵抗，参与目标设定应该会增加接受度。然而，如果参与与文化不一致，目标应该由经理分配。如果参与和文化不一致，员工可能会将参与过程视为操纵，并对其产生负面影响。', 'C': '无论目标是否可实现或是否在管理层对员工能力的认知范围内，如果员工认为目标不可实现，他们会减少努力。因此，管理者必须确保员工对自己的努力能够实现绩效目标充满信心，这意味着员工必须具备完成工作的能力，并且认为评估过程是有效的。', 'D': '不同的员工有不同的需求，经理可以根据每个员工的情况个性化地分配奖励，包括薪酬、晋升、自主权、工作范围和深度，以及参与目标设定和决策制定的机会。', 'E': '管理者需要将奖励与绩效挂钩，而不是奖励其他因素，这样只会加强这些其他因素；同时，应该通过增加奖励的可见性来最大化其影响力，例如公开通报每个人的薪酬、宣传绩效奖金以及一次性发放年薪增加等。', 'F': '不同员工群体对于奖励的重要性和评价标准存在差异，因此在分配奖励时应考虑不同员工群体的不同需求和期望。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: There is an abundance of evidence to support the motivational benefits that result from carefully matching people to jobs. For example, if the job is running a small business or an autonomous unit within a larger business, high achievers should be sought. However, if the job to be filled is a managerial post in a large bureaucratic organisation, a candidate who has a high need for power and a low need for affiliation should be selected. Accordingly, high achievers should not be put into jobs that are inconsistent with their needs. High achievers will do best when the job provides moderately challenging goals and where there is independence and feedback. However, it should be remembered that not everybody is motivated by jobs that are high in independence, variety and responsibility.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The literature on goal-setting theory suggests that managers should ensure that all employees have specific goals and receive comments on how well they are doing in those goals. For those with high achievement needs, typically a minority in any organisation, the existence of external goals is less important because high achievers are already internally motivated. The next factor to be determined is whether the goals should be assigned by a manager or collectively set in conjunction with the employees. The answer to that depends on perceptions of goal acceptance and the organisation's culture. If resistance to goals is expected, the use of participation in goal-setting should increase acceptance. If participation is inconsistent with the culture, however, goals should be assigned. If participation and the culture are incongruous, employees are likely to perceive the participation process as manipulative and be negatively affected by it.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Regardless of whether goals are achievable or well within management's perceptions of the employee's ability, if employees see them as unachievable they will reduce their effort. Managers must be sure, therefore, that employees feel confident that their efforts can lead to performance goals. For managers, this means that employees must have the capability of doing the job and must regard the appraisal process as valid.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Since employees have different needs, what acts as a reinforcement for one may not for another. Managers could use their knowledge of each employee to personalise the rewards over which they have control. Some of the more obvious rewards that managers allocate include pay, promotions, autonomy, job scope and depth, and the opportunity to participate in goal-setting and decision-making.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Managers need to make rewards contingent on performance. To reward factors other than performance will only reinforce those other factors. Key rewards such as pay increases and promotions or advancements should be allocated for the attainment of the employee's specific goals. Consistent with maximising the impact of rewards, managers should look for ways to increase their visibility. Eliminating the secrecy surrounding pay by openly communicating everyone's remuneration, publicising performance bonuses and allocating annual salary increases in a lump sum rather than spreading them out over an entire year are examples of actions that will make rewards more visible and potentially more motivating.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The way rewards are distributed should be transparent so that employees perceive that rewards or outcomes are equitable and equal to the inputs given. On a simplistic level, experience, abilities, effort and other obvious inputs should explain differences in pay, responsibility and other obvious outcomes. The problem, however, is complicated by the existence of dozens of inputs and outcomes and by the fact that employee groups place different degrees of importance on them. For instance, a study comparing clerical and production workers identified nearly twenty inputs and outcomes. The clerical workers considered factors such as quality of work performed and job knowledge near the top of their list, but these were at the bottom of the production workers' list. Similarly, production workers thought that the most important inputs were intelligence and personal involvement with task accomplishment, two factors that were quite low in the importance ratings of the clerks. There were also important, though less dramatic, differences on the outcome side. For example, production workers rated advancement very highly, whereas clerical workers rated advancement in the lower third of their list. Such findings suggest that one person's equity is another's inequity, so an ideal should probably weigh different inputs and outcomes according to employee group.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14-18

Reading Passage 2 contains six Key Points.
Choose the correct heading for Key Points TWO to SIX from the list of headings below and move it into the gap.

Example Answer
Key Point One viii - Ensure employees are suited to their jobs

list.text: Ensure the reward system is fair
Encourage managers to take more responsibility
Recognise changes in employees' performance over time
Ensure employees are suited to their jobs

questions.analysis: Section B的内容主要讨论了设定具体目标和给予反馈的重要性。关键句子提到“specific goals”和“receive comments”，这与标题vii中的“Establish targets”和“give feedback”高度对应。因此，标题vii是最合适的选择。

central_sentences.sentence: The literature on goal-setting theory suggests that managers should ensure that all employees have specific goals and receive comments on how well they are doing in those goals.

groups.desc:
Questions 19-24

Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: A shrinking organisation tends to lose its less skilled employees rather than its more skilled employees.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述的是 "A shrinking organisation tends to lose its less skilled employees rather than its more skilled employees."，意思是缩减中的组织倾向于失去技能较低的员工而不是技能较高的员工。然而，原文明确指出，当组织缩减时，最优秀和最具流动性的员工（即技能较高的员工）更倾向于自愿离开，而技能较低的员工则因为工作选择有限而留下。因此，题干的陈述与原文内容相反，正确答案是 "NO"。

central_sentences.sentence: When an organisation is shrinking, the best and most mobile workers are prone to leave voluntarily. Unfortunately, they are the ones the organisation can least afford to lose - those with the highest skills and experience. The minor employees remain because their job options are limited.
central_sentences.translation: 当一个组织正在缩减时，最优秀和最有流动性的员工倾向于自愿离开。不幸的是，他们恰恰是组织最不愿失去的人——那些技能和经验最高的人。次要员工则因为工作选择有限而留下。

target_sen.target_sen: When an organisation is shrinking, the best and most mobile workers are prone to leave voluntarily.
The minor employees remain because their job options are limited.

syn_ary.synonym: the best and most mobile workers

target_sen.target_sen: When an organisation is shrinking, the best and most mobile workers are prone to leave voluntarily.

syn_ary.synonym: those with the highest skills and experience

target_sen.target_sen: Unfortunately, they are the ones the organisation can least afford to lose - those with the highest skills and experience.

questions.title: It is easier to manage a small business than a large business.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“easier”（更容易）和“manage”（管理）在原文中没有直接的同义替换词来表示管理小企业比大企业更容易。原文只是讨论了不同类型的工作需要不同类型的员工，但并没有明确比较管理小企业和大企业的难易程度。因此，答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供明确的信息来支持或反对题干的说法。

central_sentences.sentence: For example, if the job is running a small business or an autonomous unit within a larger business, high achievers should be sought. However, if the job to be filled is a managerial post in a large bureaucratic organisation, a candidate who has a high need for power and a low need for affiliation should be selected.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，如果工作是经营一个小企业或者大企业中的一个自主单位，应该寻找高成就者。然而，如果要填补的是大型官僚组织中的管理职位，应该选择那些有高权力需求和低亲和需求的候选人。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, if the job is running a small business or an autonomous unit within a larger business, high achievers should be sought.
However, if the job to be filled is a managerial post in a large bureaucratic organisation, a candidate who has a high need for power and a low need for affiliation should be selected.
For example, if the job is running a small business or an autonomous unit within a larger business, high achievers should be sought.
However, if the job to be filled is a managerial post in a large bureaucratic organisation, a candidate who has a high need for power and a low need for affiliation should be selected.

questions.title: High achievers are well suited to team work.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“High achievers”在原文中直接对应“high achievers”，而“team work”在原文中对应的是“independence”。原文明确指出高成就者在有独立性和反馈的环境中表现最好，这与团队合作的要求相反。因此，题干中的陈述“High achievers are well suited to team work”是错误的，正确答案为NO。

central_sentences.sentence: High achievers will do best when the job provides moderately challenging goals and where there is independence and feedback.
central_sentences.translation: 高成就者在工作提供适度挑战性目标且有独立性和反馈的情况下表现最佳。

target_sen.target_sen: High achievers will do best when the job provides moderately challenging goals and where there is independence and feedback.
High achievers will do best when the job provides moderately challenging goals and where there is independence and feedback.
However, it should be remembered that not everybody is motivated by jobs that are high in independence, variety and responsibility.

questions.title: Some employees can feel manipulated when asked to participate in goal-setting.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Some employees can feel manipulated when asked to participate in goal-setting.”在原文中得到了明确的对应。原文中提到“employees are likely to perceive the participation process as manipulative”，这与题干中的“feel manipulated”是同义表达。同时，原文中的“participation in goal-setting”与题干中的“participate in goal-setting”也是一致的。因此，题干中的信息在原文中可以找到，答案为YES。

central_sentences.sentence: If participation and the culture are incongruous, employees are likely to perceive the participation process as manipulative and be negatively affected by it.
central_sentences.translation: 如果参与过程与组织文化不一致，员工很可能会认为参与过程是操纵性的，并因此受到负面影响。

target_sen.target_sen: If participation and the culture are incongruous, employees are likely to perceive the participation process as manipulative and be negatively affected by it.
If resistance to goals is expected, the use of participation in goal-setting should increase acceptance.
If participation is inconsistent with the culture, however, goals should be assigned.
If participation and the culture are incongruous, employees are likely to perceive the participation process as manipulative and be negatively affected by it.
The literature on goal-setting theory suggests that managers should ensure that all employees have specific goals and receive comments on how well they are doing in those goals.
If resistance to goals is expected, the use of participation in goal-setting should increase acceptance.

questions.title: The staff appraisal process should be designed by employees.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 "NOT GIVEN"，因为文章中并没有提到评估过程是否应该由员工设计。题干中的核心信息 "staff appraisal process" 和 "should be designed by employees" 在文章中没有明确的对应或反驳。文章提到的是管理者需要确保员工对评估过程的信任和认可，但并没有提到评估过程应该由员工设计。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的支持或反对的内容。

central_sentences.sentence: Managers must be sure, therefore, that employees feel confident that their efforts can lead to performance goals. For managers, this means that employees must have the capability of doing the job and must regard the appraisal process as valid.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，管理者必须确保员工有信心相信他们的努力能够达到绩效目标。对于管理者来说，这意味着员工必须具备完成工作的能力，并且必须认为评估过程是有效的。

target_sen.target_sen: For managers, this means that employees must have the capability of doing the job and must regard the appraisal process as valid.

questions.title: Employees' earnings should be disclosed to everyone within the organisation.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Employees' earnings should be disclosed to everyone within the organisation" 与原文中的 "Eliminating the secrecy surrounding pay by openly communicating everyone's remuneration" 意思相同。题干中的 "earnings" 对应原文中的 "remuneration"，"disclosed" 对应 "openly communicating"，"everyone" 在两者中都出现。因此，题干信息在文章中找到了对应，答案为 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Eliminating the secrecy surrounding pay by openly communicating everyone's remuneration, publicising performance bonuses and allocating annual salary increases in a lump sum rather than spreading them out over an entire year are examples of actions that will make rewards more visible and potentially more motivating.
central_sentences.translation: 通过公开交流每个人的薪酬来消除围绕工资的保密性，公布绩效奖金，并将年度工资增长一次性发放而不是分散在整年，这些都是使奖励更加可见且可能更具激励作用的做法的例子。

target_sen.target_sen: Eliminating the secrecy surrounding pay by openly communicating everyone's remuneration, publicising performance bonuses and allocating annual salary increases in a lump sum rather than spreading them out over an entire year are examples of actions that will make rewards more visible and potentially more motivating.

Eliminating the secrecy surrounding pay by openly communicating everyone's remuneration, publicising performance bonuses and allocating annual salary increases in a lump sum rather than spreading them out over an entire year are examples of actions that will make rewards more visible and potentially more motivating.

Questions 25-27
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: They judge promotion to be important.
They have less need of external goals.
They think that the quality of their work is important.
They resist goals which are imposed.

For example, production workers rated advancement very highly, whereas clerical workers rated advancement in the lower third of their list.

central_sentences.sentence: For example, production workers rated advancement very highly, whereas clerical workers rated advancement in the lower third of their list.

target_sen.target_sen: For instance, a study comparing clerical and production workers identified nearly twenty inputs and outcomes.
The clerical workers considered factors such as quality of work performed and job knowledge near the top of their list, but these were at the bottom of the production workers' list.

Similarly, production workers thought that the most important inputs were intelligence and personal involvement with task accomplishment, two factors that were quite low in the importance ratings of the clerks.

content.title: The Search for the Anti-aging Pill
content.notes: ['* calorie: a measure of the energy value of food']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: As researchers on aging noted recently, no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging - the build-up of molecular and cellular damage that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older. But one intervention, consumption of a low-calorie* yet nutritionally balanced diet, works incredibly well in a broad range of animals, increasing longevity and prolonging good health. Those findings suggest that caloric restriction could delay aging and increase longevity in humans, too.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Unfortunately, for maximum benefit, people would probably have to reduce their caloric intake by roughly thirty percent, equivalent to dropping from 2,500 calories a day to 1,750. Few mortals could stick to that harsh a regimen, especially for years on end. But what if someone could create a pill that mimicked the physiological effects of eating less without actually forcing people to eat less? Could such a 'caloric-restriction mimetic', as we call it, enable people to stay healthy longer, postponing age-related disorders (such as diabetes, arteriosclerosis, heart disease and cancer) until very late in life? Scientists first posed this question in the mid-1990s, after researchers came upon a chemical agent that in rodents seemed to reproduce many of caloric restriction's benefits. No compound that would safely achieve the same feat in people has been found yet, but the search has been informative and has fanned hope that caloric-restriction (CR) mimetics can indeed be developed eventually.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
The benefits of caloric restriction
The hunt for CR mimetics grew out of a desire to better understand caloric restriction's many effects on the body. Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago, when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions that become increasingly common in old age. What is more, some of the treated animals survived longer than the oldest-living animals in the control group, which means that the maximum lifespan (the oldest attainable age), not merely the normal lifespan, increased. Various interventions, such as infection-fighting drugs, can increase a population's average survival time, but only approaches that slow the body's rate of aging will increase the maximum lifespan.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The rat findings have been replicated many times and extended to creatures ranging from yeast to fruit flies, worms, fish, spiders, mice and hamsters. Until fairly recently, the studies were limited to short-lived creatures genetically distant from humans. But caloric-restriction projects underway in two species more closely related to humans - rhesus and squirrel monkeys - have made scientists optimistic that CR mimetics could help people.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The monkey projects demonstrate that, compared with control animals that eat normally, caloric-restricted monkeys have lower body temperatures and levels of the pancreatic hormone insulin, and they retain more youthful levels of certain hormones that tend to fall with age.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The caloric-restricted animals also look better on indicators of risk for age-related diseases. For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and they have more normal blood glucose levels (pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels). Further, it has recently been shown that rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years) have less chronic disease. They and the other monkeys must be followed still longer, however, to know whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys. Unlike the multitude of elixirs being touted as the latest anti-aging cure, CR mimetics would alter fundamental processes that underlie aging. We aim to develop compounds that fool cells into activating maintenance and repair.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text:
How a prototype caloric-restriction mimetic works
The best-studied candidate for a caloric-restriction mimetic, 2DG (2-deoxy-D-glucose), works by interfering with the way cells process glucose. It has proved toxic at some doses in animals and so cannot be used in humans. But it has demonstrated that chemicals can replicate the effects of caloric restriction; the trick is finding the right one.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Cells use the glucose from food to generate ATP (adenosine triphosphate), the molecule that powers many activities in the body. By limiting food intake, caloric restriction minimizes the amount of glucose entering cells and decreases ATP generation. When 2DG is administered to animals that eat normally, glucose reaches cells in abundance but the drug prevents most of it from being processed and thus reduces ATP synthesis. Researchers have proposed several explanations for why interruption of glucose processing and ATP production might retard aging. One possibility relates to the ATP-making machinery's emission of free radicals, which are thought to contribute to aging and to such age-related diseases as cancer by damaging cells. Reduced operation of the machinery should limit their production and thereby constrain the damage. Another hypothesis suggests that decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction.

groups.desc:
Questions 28-32

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: Studies show drugs available today can delay the process of growing old.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Studies show drugs available today can delay the process of growing old”意思是“研究表明目前有的药物可以延缓衰老过程”。在文章中，第一段明确提到“no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging”，意思是“目前市场上没有任何治疗方法被证明能延缓人类衰老”。这与题干信息相反，因此正确答案是“NO”。

central_sentences.sentence: As researchers on aging noted recently, no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging - the build-up of molecular and cellular damage that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older.
central_sentences.translation: 正如衰老研究人员最近指出的，目前市场上还没有任何治疗方法被证明能延缓人类衰老——即随着我们年龄增长，分子和细胞损伤的积累使我们更容易出现虚弱。

target_sen.target_sen: As researchers on aging noted recently, no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging - the build-up of molecular and cellular damage that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older.

As researchers on aging noted recently, no treatment on the market today has been proved to slow human aging - the build-up of molecular and cellular damage that increases vulnerability to infirmity as we grow older.

locating_words.locating_word: delay the process of growing old

questions.title: There is scientific evidence that eating fewer calories may extend human life.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“scientific evidence”对应原文中的“findings”，“eating fewer calories”对应“consumption of a low-calorie diet”，“extend human life”对应“increase longevity in humans”。原文明确提到这些研究结果表明限制卡路里摄入可能延缓人类衰老并增加寿命，因此答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Those findings suggest that caloric restriction could delay aging and increase longevity in humans, too.
central_sentences.translation: 这些发现表明，热量限制也可能延缓人类的衰老过程并增加寿命。

syn_ary.synonym: consumption of a low-calorie yet nutritionally balanced diet

target_sen.target_sen: But one intervention, consumption of a low-calorie* yet nutritionally balanced diet, works incredibly well in a broad range of animals, increasing longevity and prolonging good health.
Those findings suggest that caloric restriction could delay aging and increase longevity in humans, too.

questions.title: Not many people are likely to find a caloric-restricted diet attractive.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Not many people" 和 "caloric-restricted diet attractive" 在原文中分别对应 "Few mortals" 和 "stick to that harsh a regimen"。原文明确指出很少有人能够坚持这种严格的饮食计划，尤其是长期坚持，这表明大多数人不会觉得这种饮食有吸引力。因此，题干的陈述是正确的，答案为 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Few mortals could stick to that harsh a regimen, especially for years on end.
central_sentences.translation: 很少有人能坚持这种严格的饮食计划，尤其是长年累月地坚持下去。

target_sen.target_sen: Few mortals could stick to that harsh a regimen, especially for years on end.
Few mortals could stick to that harsh a regimen, especially for years on end.

questions.title: Diet-related diseases are common in older people.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Diet-related diseases”在原文中并没有明确提到，虽然提到了“age-related disorders”（与年龄相关的疾病），但并没有说这些疾病是由饮食引起的。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的对应，答案应为NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 题干中的“diet-related diseases”在文中无对应，仅提及“age-related diseases”
central_sentences.analysis: 文中未提“饮食相关疾病”在老年人中是否常见。

questions.title: In experiments, rats who ate what they wanted led shorter lives than rats on a low-calorie diet.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "rats who ate what they wanted" 对应文章中的 "free-feeding rats"，"led shorter lives" 对应文章中的 "lived longer on average than"。文章明确指出，喂食低热量饮食的老鼠比自由进食的老鼠寿命更长，这与题干中的信息完全一致。因此，答案为 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago, when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions that become increasingly common in old age.
central_sentences.translation: 60多年前，科学家首次认识到这种做法的价值，他们发现食用低热量饮食的老鼠平均寿命比自由进食的老鼠更长，而且随着年龄增长而变得常见的疾病发生率也降低了。

target_sen.target_sen: Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago, when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions that become increasingly common in old age.

Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago, when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions that become increasingly common in old age.

Scientists first recognized the value of the practice more than 60 years ago, when they found that rats fed a low-calorie diet lived longer on average than free-feeding rats and also had a reduced incidence of conditions that become increasingly common in old age.

groups.title: Classify the following descriptions as relating to
groups.desc:
Questions 33-37

Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

collect_option.title: Classify the following descriptions as relating to

list.text: neither caloric-restricted monkeys nor control monkeys

questions.title: Monkeys were less likely to become diabetic.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Monkeys were less likely to become diabetic”意思是“猴子患糖尿病的可能性较低”。在原文中，提到“caloric-restricted animals”有“more normal blood glucose levels”，这表明它们的糖尿病风险降低。由此可以确定，热量限制的猴子患糖尿病的可能性较低，因此正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: The caloric-restricted animals also look better on indicators of risk for age-related diseases. For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and they have more normal blood glucose levels (pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels).
central_sentences.translation: 热量限制的动物在年龄相关疾病的风险指标上表现更好。例如，它们有更低的血压和甘油三酯水平（表明心脏病的可能性降低），并且血糖水平更正常（表明糖尿病的风险降低，糖尿病的特征是异常高的血糖水平）。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and they have more normal blood glucose levels (pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels).
The caloric-restricted animals also look better on indicators of risk for age-related diseases.

questions.title: Monkeys experienced more chronic disease.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Monkeys experienced more chronic disease”意思是“猴子经历了更多的慢性疾病”。在原文中，提到“rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets... have less chronic disease”，意思是“保持热量限制饮食的恒河猴患慢性疾病的情况更少”。因此，可以推断出对照组猴子（control monkeys）患慢性疾病的情况更多，所以正确答案是“B: control monkeys”。

central_sentences.sentence: Further, it has recently been shown that rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years) have less chronic disease.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，最近的研究表明，长期（近15年）接受热量限制饮食的恒河猴患慢性疾病的概率更低。

target_sen.target_sen: Further, it has recently been shown that rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years) have less chronic disease.
They and the other monkeys must be followed still longer, however, to know whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys.
Further, it has recently been shown that rhesus monkeys kept on caloric-restricted diets for an extended time (nearly 15 years) have less chronic disease.

questions.title: Monkeys have been shown to experience a longer than average life span.
questions.analysis: 题干问的是猴子是否已经显示出比平均寿命更长的情况。文章明确表示目前还不确定低热量饮食是否能延长寿命，所以正确答案是 "C: neither caloric-restricted monkeys nor control monkeys"。这说明无论是低热量摄入的猴子还是控制组猴子，都没有被证明寿命更长。

central_sentences.sentence: They and the other monkeys must be followed still longer, however, to know whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，这些猴子和其他猴子还需要继续观察更长时间，以确定低热量摄入是否能增加猴子的平均寿命和最大寿命。

syn_ary.synonym: increase both average and maximum lifespans

target_sen.target_sen: They and the other monkeys must be followed still longer, however, to know whether low-calorie intake can increase both average and maximum lifespans in monkeys.

questions.title: Monkeys enjoyed a reduced chance of heart disease.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“reduced chance of heart disease”在原文中对应的是“decreased likelihood of heart disease”，而“Monkeys”对应的是“caloric-restricted monkeys”。原文明确指出热量限制的动物在心脏病风险指标上表现更好，因此答案是“A: caloric-restricted monkeys”。

central_sentences.sentence: The caloric-restricted animals also look better on indicators of risk for age-related diseases. For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and
central_sentences.translation: 热量限制的动物在年龄相关疾病的风险指标上表现更好。例如，它们的血压和甘油三酯水平较低（表明患心脏病的可能性降低）。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and they have more normal blood glucose levels (pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels).

For example, they have lower blood pressure and triglyceride levels (signifying a decreased likelihood of heart disease), and they have more normal blood glucose levels (pointing to a reduced risk for diabetes, which is marked by unusually high blood glucose levels).

questions.title: Monkeys produced greater quantities of insulin.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“greater quantities of insulin”对应原文中的“higher levels of insulin”。原文明确指出，热量限制的猴子胰岛素水平较低，这意味着对照组猴子的胰岛素水平较高。因此，正确答案是B: control monkeys。

central_sentences.sentence: The monkey projects demonstrate that, compared with control animals that eat normally, caloric-restricted monkeys have lower body temperatures and levels of the pancreatic hormone insulin, and they retain more youthful levels of certain hormones that tend to fall with age.
central_sentences.translation: 猴子实验项目表明，与正常进食的对照组动物相比，热量限制的猴子具有更低的体温和胰腺激素胰岛素水平，并且保持了某些随年龄增长而趋于下降的荷尔蒙的更年轻水平。

target_sen.target_sen: The monkey projects demonstrate that, compared with control animals that eat normally, caloric-restricted monkeys have lower body temperatures and levels of the pancreatic hormone insulin, and they retain more youthful levels of certain hormones that tend to fall with age.

groups.title: How a caloric-restriction mimetic works
groups.desc:
Questions 38-40

Complete the flow-chart below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the text in each gap.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“less ... is processed”对应原文中的“prevents most of it from being processed”。在这句话中，“it”指代的是前一句中的“glucose”，即葡萄糖。因此，答案是“glucose”。

central_sentences.sentence: When 2DG is administered to animals that eat normally, glucose reaches cells in abundance but the drug prevents most of it from being processed and thus reduces ATP synthesis.
central_sentences.translation: 当2DG被给予正常饮食的动物时，葡萄糖大量进入细胞，但药物阻止了大部分葡萄糖被处理，从而减少了ATP的合成。

syn_ary.synonym: prevents most of it from being processed

target_sen.target_sen: When 2DG is administered to animals that eat normally, glucose reaches cells in abundance but the drug prevents most of it from being processed and thus reduces ATP synthesis.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "fewer" 和 "emitted" 是关键线索词。在原文中，"fewer" 对应 "limit"，"emitted" 对应 "emission"。通过这些同义词的对应关系，可以定位到答案句 "Reduced operation of the machinery should limit their production and thereby constrain the damage."。在这句话中，"free radicals" 是被限制排放的对象，因此答案是 "free radicals"。

central_sentences.sentence: One possibility relates to the ATP-making machinery's emission of free radicals, which are thought to contribute to aging and to such age-related diseases as cancer by damaging cells. Reduced operation of the machinery should limit their production and thereby constrain the damage.
central_sentences.translation: 一种可能性与ATP制造机器的自由基排放有关，自由基被认为通过损害细胞导致衰老和癌症等与年龄相关的疾病。机器的减少运作应该会限制它们的产生，从而减少损害。

target_sen.target_sen: By limiting food intake, caloric restriction minimizes the amount of glucose entering cells and decreases ATP generation.
Reduced operation of the machinery should limit their production and thereby constrain the damage.
One possibility relates to the ATP-making machinery's emission of free radicals, which are thought to contribute to aging and to such age-related diseases as cancer by damaging cells.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "cells focus on 40______ because food is in short supply" 对应文章中的 "decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction." 这里的 "emphasizes preservation" 和 "focus on preservation" 是同义表达。因此，正确答案是 "preservation"。

central_sentences.sentence: decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction.
central_sentences.translation: 葡萄糖处理减少可能会向细胞表明食物稀缺（即使实际上并非如此），并促使它们转入一种强调保存有机体而非生长和繁殖的抗衰老模式。

target_sen.target_sen: Another hypothesis suggests that decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction.

Another hypothesis suggests that decreased processing of glucose could indicate to cells that food is scarce (even if it isn't) and induce them to shift into an anti-aging mode that emphasizes preservation of the organism over such 'luxuries' as growth and reproduction.
